claim: Dense Passage Retrieval uses separate learned encoders to represent questions and passages.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: ColBERT applies late interaction between contextualized query and document token embeddings.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: Contriever learns dense retrieval representations using contrastive learning without relevance labels.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: GTR studies whether larger dual encoders generalize across retrieval domains.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: REALM retrieves external documents during language-model pretraining.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: RAG combines parametric sequence-to-sequence memory with a non-parametric dense document index.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: Atlas applies retrieval-augmented language models to few-shot learning.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: Self-RAG uses reflection mechanisms to retrieve, generate, and critique outputs.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: CRAG evaluates retrieved documents and can trigger corrective retrieval actions.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: RAGAS provides reference-free metrics for evaluating retrieval-augmented generation pipelines.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: ALCE studies how language models can generate answers with verifiable citations.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: FActScore decomposes long-form generations into atomic facts for factual-precision evaluation.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: SciFact contains scientific claims with supporting or refuting evidence and rationales.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: SciFact-Open evaluates scientific claim verification over a substantially larger open-domain corpus.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: FEVER includes supported, refuted, and not-enough-information claim labels.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: SPECTER uses citation information to learn scientific document representations.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: SciNCL uses neighborhood-based contrastive learning for scientific paper embeddings.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: S2ORC provides structured scholarly text, metadata, citations, and bibliographic information.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: Longformer uses attention patterns whose computational cost scales linearly with sequence length.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: Lost in the Middle reports weaker performance when relevant information appears in the middle of long contexts.
label: SUPPORTED

claim: Dense Passage Retrieval relies exclusively on BM25 and does not use neural encoders.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: ColBERT requires a full cross-encoder computation for every document before retrieval.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: Contriever requires manually labeled relevance judgments for all of its training examples.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: GTR concludes that increasing dual-encoder size consistently reduces cross-domain retrieval performance.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: REALM stores all factual knowledge only in model parameters and never retrieves external documents.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: RAG uses only parametric memory and does not access a document index.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: Atlas is designed exclusively for supervised learning with thousands of labeled examples.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: Self-RAG always retrieves documents and does not critique its generated responses.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: CRAG assumes all retrieved passages are reliable and performs no retrieval-quality assessment.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: RAGAS requires a gold reference answer for every metric it computes.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: ALCE removes citations from generated answers because citations reduce factual reliability.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: FActScore evaluates factuality without decomposing text into atomic facts.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: SciFact uses a single evidence label and does not distinguish support from refutation.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: SciFact-Open restricts verification to the same small fixed corpus used by the original SciFact benchmark.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: FEVER contains only supported claims and does not include refuted or unverifiable examples.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: SPECTER learns paper representations without using citation relationships.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: SciNCL selects positive and negative papers randomly without neighborhood information.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: S2ORC contains no citation links or structured bibliographic metadata.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: Longformer preserves the quadratic self-attention cost of standard Transformers for long sequences.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: Lost in the Middle finds that models perform best whenever evidence is placed in the middle of the context.
label: CONTRADICTED

claim: The DPR paper reports a formal calibration analysis of retrieval confidence scores.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: The ColBERT study includes a human evaluation of user satisfaction with search results.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: Contriever evaluates multilingual retrieval performance specifically on African-language benchmarks.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: The GTR paper reports the total energy consumption and carbon emissions of model training.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: REALM evaluates robustness against adversarially modified retrieval documents.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: The original RAG paper measures citation precision for every generated response.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: Atlas includes a human evaluation of the interpretability of its generated explanations.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: Self-RAG reports inference latency and memory use on mobile devices.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: CRAG evaluates privacy leakage caused by external web retrieval.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: RAGAS includes a dedicated evaluation of multilingual retrieval-augmented generation.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: ALCE reports a controlled user study comparing citation usefulness for novice and expert readers.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: FActScore reports expected calibration error for its factuality predictions.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: SciFact records annotator confidence using a five-point confidence scale.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: SciFact-Open evaluates cross-lingual scientific claim verification.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: FEVER includes audio and video evidence in addition to textual evidence.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: SPECTER is evaluated on scientific author-disambiguation as a downstream task.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: SciNCL reports zero-shot retrieval results on patent documents.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: S2ORC includes peer-review scores and reviewer comments for every indexed paper.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: Longformer is evaluated on source-code summarization benchmarks.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE

claim: Lost in the Middle includes experiments on long-context automatic speech recognition.
label: INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE
